basic_info.title: Metal Gear
basic_info.review: Metal Gear is a groundbreaking stealth game that pioneered the genre and established many conventions still used today. While the NES port has significant differences from Kojima's original vision, it remains a beloved classic that influenced countless games.

play_today.details: Modern PC port with widescreen support, save states, and no region lock. Best for accessibility and ease of play.

recommended_patches.name: Metal Gear Solid HD Collection Patch
recommended_patches.description: Enhanced graphics and widescreen support from the HD Collection port

play_today.platform: Nintendo Switch / PlayStation 4 / PlayStation 5 / Xbox Series X/S

Original canonical version by Hideo Kojima. Requires MSX emulator with tape drive support for save functionality.

play_today.details: Original Western release with significant changes from Kojima's vision. Includes fan translation patches for improved localization.

play_today.details: 1989 port based on NES version. Limited availability but historically significant.
1990 port based on NES version. Rare and historically interesting but not recommended for modern play.

description.synopsis: Solid Snake, a special forces operative, infiltrates the fortified state of Outer Heaven to destroy Metal Gear, a bipedal walking tank capable of launching nuclear missiles, while rescuing captured allies and uncovering a conspiracy.
key_features: Radio transceiver for storytelling through dialogue
Multiple difficulty levels based on hostage rescue count
description.long_description: Metal Gear, developed by Konami and designed by Hideo Kojima, is widely recognized as the first mainstream stealth game. Released in 1987 for MSX2 in Japan, it introduced revolutionary gameplay mechanics where players must avoid detection rather than engage enemies directly. The game features a portable radio transceiver that enables organic storytelling through dialogue with allies and enemies. Despite being developed without Kojima's direct involvement, the NES port became a major international success, selling 1 million units in the United States alone. The MSX2 version is considered the canonical version by Kojima himself, while the NES version features significant alterations including different level designs and the replacement of the Metal Gear boss with a supercomputer. Both versions have been preserved through various ports and remasters, including the GOG.com PC release in 2020 and inclusion in the Metal Gear Solid: Master Collection Vol. 1 for modern platforms.
legacy_and_impact: Introduced innovative storytelling via radio transceiver
Influenced countless games including Splinter Cell, Metal Gear Solid series, and more
Considered one of the greatest games ever made
NES version sold 1 million units in the US alone
